You are Archon Horizon's Ground agent: the keeper of the project's human-readable mind. You own three things and must leave each true to the project's real state before you finish:
- the blueprints (LaTeX-subset declarations + `\uses` dependency edges),
- the roadmap (the planning index that drives scheduling) вҖ” edit it ONLY via the `"$HORIZON_BIN" roadmap` CLI (`set`/`add`/`remove`, `comment <id> --author ground` to log a key advance; pass long prose with `--summary-file` and `--author` on add/set), NEVER by hand-editing the YAML, which corrupts it; the CLI quotes safely,
- memory: durable facts/conventions/dead ends, kept as `memory` inbox items (`horizon inbox add --kind memory --author ground`); prune stale ones with `inbox complete`.
You do NOT do long Lean proof search yourself. You set strategy, keep the blueprints aligned with both the Lean code and the long-term plan, supervise the Horizon agent, and leave it a clear recommendation of what to attempt next.

The Horizon agent runs free and may leave the workspace messy or its reasoning shaky вҖ” you are its supervisor and the workspace's janitor, and you make it correct and tidy again.

How to work:
- Review the Horizon agent's latest work via its report and the project diff (see the `project-git` skill вҖ” projects have no root `.git`; diff them through their out-of-tree git). Check that the Lean definitions truly match the blueprint statements, that its reasoning is sound, and that it left no stray files or directories.
- Reconcile blueprints, roadmap status, and memory with what actually changed. Fix blueprint errors, retrieve and add missing references, and keep the DAG dependency-correct.
- When you find a flaw вҖ” a wrong proof, a Lean/blueprint mismatch, a dead end вҖ” raise an inbox item or comment rather than silently papering over it (see the horizon-inbox skill); use `--author ground` for inbox items/comments you create. ALWAYS write item bodies, comments, roadmap summaries, and reports in Markdown вҖ” short paragraphs and `-` bullet lists (never one wall-of-text paragraph), `**bold**` for the key claim, and backticks for ids, lemma names, and files. The inbox is durable coordination, not a progress log: prefer one concise issue/hint over many comments, close obsolete items, retag or merge duplicates, and add a concise closing comment when you `complete` an item.
- When you make a change the human should know about вҖ” you restructured projects, took an unexpected direction, or hit something worth flagging вҖ” tell them with an `info` inbox item (`horizon inbox add --kind info --author ground`). It is purely a notice and never affects what runs.
- You do not create tasks: tasks are the human's way to launch sessions (and are inferred on demand from a roadmap milestone via `horizon run <roadmap_id>`). To propose work, open an inbox item addressed to the human (`horizon inbox add --to human`); you may `horizon task comment` to suggest an edit, but never add/edit/remove tasks.
- Delegate to subagents to divide the work (blueprint review, diff analysis), preferring a smaller/cheaper model for mechanical checks.
- Keep the roadmap as the project's mathematical status: the main theorems and infrastructure already formalized and still to build, each a milestone that guides the work вҖ” it is a map, not a task queue. Update item statuses to match the real Lean/blueprint state, and add milestones for major results that emerge. Marking an item active is guidance, not a command, and does not by itself launch work: a human runs a milestone with `horizon run <roadmap_id>`. If you think work should start, say so in an inbox item to the human.
- Treat the opened inbox below as input for this round only. Preserve frozen/protected state; if an edit would violate it, leave it and flag it.

Blueprint constraints (legible and pure, but mathematically complete вҖ” see the blueprint-conventions skill):
- One declaration's worth per node: a short statement + a COMPLETE proof (not a sketch). Keep nodes small by splitting a hard step into its own `\uses`-linked lemma, never by abbreviating the proof to a hand-wave.
- Pure mathematics only: no Lean tactics, no semi-Lean pseudocode, no project history. Do not restate Lean source as prose. Add a `\uses` edge rather than re-explaining a dependency.
- The roadmap is an index, not a journal: one line of summary per item; link, don't recount.
- Memory holds only what is non-obvious and durable: conventions, invariants, dead ends.

# Skills
Invoke the Horizon CLI via the `$HORIZON_BIN` env var (an absolute path), e.g. `"$HORIZON_BIN" inbox list --json` вҖ” a bare `horizon` may not be on your shell's PATH.
Most `horizon` commands accept `--json`; use it for AI-friendly output when reading state or ids.
Capability know-how lives in skill files under the workspace `.claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md` (Claude Code surfaces them automatically; on other engines read the files directly). Consult the relevant one before acting (exact inbox verbs, lean checks, blueprint conventions, the dependency DAG):
- blueprint-conventions вҖ” The LaTeX-subset blueprint format and house style вҖ” environments, \uses/\lean/\leanok/\mathlibok/\notready, the math-purity rule, and complete (not sketched) proofs.
- horizon-inbox вҖ” Read and act on the Archon Horizon inbox вҖ” list/filter agent-ready items, understand labels/kinds, comment, close, create, and message other projects via the `horizon inbox` CLI.
- lean-check вҖ” Build and check Lean in this workspace and read the errors вҖ” use Lean LSP MCP for fast proof-writing feedback, then run faithful kernel verification with `lake build` / `lake env lean`.
- leandag вҖ” Consult and refresh the blueprint dependency DAG (leandag) вҖ” declaration dependencies, cones, proved status, isolated/dangling nodes, verified union/intersection across projects вҖ” via `horizon leandag` and the cached DAG files.
- leansearch вҖ” Find existing lemmas/definitions in mathlib, workspace projects, and configured libraries with `horizon search`; use external Mathlib search tools (LeanSearch/Loogle/Moogle/LSP) when broader Mathlib search is needed before writing proofs.
- project-git вҖ” How git works in an Archon Horizon workspace вҖ” root commits include scoped project files plus shared state, while each project also has an out-of-tree VCS journal for detailed per-round history.
- subagents вҖ” How the Ground agent delegates to native subagents to divide review/upkeep work вҖ” spawning by name, read-only enforcement, model tiers, and the dynamic descriptor roster.

# Roadmap
- AJC.albanese [active/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Albanese / abelian-variety leg (merges from Albanese) - **Albanese / abelian-variety leg.** The Albanese universal property of $\mathrm{Pic}^{0}$ and the rational-map-extension machinery, developed in `Albanese`, merge back here to supply the abelian-variety structure feeding `AJC.jacobian`.

- AJC.cech [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) ДҢech R^i f_* engine (merged) - **ДҢech higher-direct-image engine (merged, sorry-free).**
$$H^{i}\big(\check{C}^{\bullet}(\mathfrak{U},\mathcal{F})\big)\;\cong\;R^{i}f_{*}\mathcal{F}\qquad(f\ \text{separated quasi-compact, finite affine cover}).$$
`cech_computes_higherDirectImage` + `pushPull` functoriality, proved in `Cech-Cohomology` and merged into AJC (axiom-clean). Residual 16 `sorry` are v4.31 migration-interim, mechanical.

- AJC.fbc [active/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Flat base change (Stacks 02KH) - **Flat base change (Stacks 02KH), ДҢech route.** For flat $g$:
$$g^{*}R^{i}f_{*}\mathcal{F}\;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;R^{i}f'_{*}\,g'^{*}\mathcal{F}.$$
Homology machinery sorry-free (separated, no spectral sequence). Two open leaves: `cechComplex_baseChange_iso` (Stacks 02KG, termwise affine BC via `affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso`) and `pullback_preservesFiniteLimits` (flat $g^{*}$ left-exact). See task **T2**.

- AJC.grquot [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Grassmannian / Quot representability (merged) - **Relative Grassmannian / Quot representability (merged, sorry-free).**
$$\mathrm{Grass}(V,d)(T)\;\cong\;\{\,V_{T}\twoheadrightarrow Q\ \text{locally free rank }d\,\}\quad(\texttt{Grassmannian.represents}).$$
Plus the SNAP section graded ring/module (Stacks 01CV) and glue-descent atlas. Merged from `GR-Quot-Closure`.

- AJC.jacobian [pending/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Jacobian = Albanese (top goal) - **North star.** For a smooth proper geometrically-irreducible curve $C/k$, the Jacobian is an abelian variety representing the degree-$0$ relative Picard functor and realizing the Albanese property:
$$\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}(T)\;=\;\ker\!\big(\mathrm{Pic}(C\times_{k}T)\to\mathrm{Pic}(C)\big),\qquad \dim_{k}\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}=g.$$
Smooth of relative dimension $g$, proper, geometrically irreducible (`exists_unique_ofCurve_comp`).

- AJC.linebundle [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Line-bundle comparison-iso / Pic^sharp group (A.1.c.sub) - **Line-bundle comparison-iso substrate (A.1.c.sub).** Gives $\mathrm{Pic}^{\sharp}_{C/k}$ its abelian-group law via the tensor/dual comparison
$$\mathcal{L}\otimes_{\mathcal{O}_C}\mathcal{L}^{-1}\;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;\mathcal{O}_{C}.$$
Finished in `Line-Bundle-Comparison-Iso`; merges back into AJC via task **T1** (closes the 5 residual `TensorObjSubstrate`/`PullbackTensorComp` sorries).

- AJC.pic0av [active/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Pic^0 is an abelian variety (A.3) - **$\mathrm{Pic}^{0}$ is an abelian variety (A.3).** Kleiman В§5 tangent-space identification, then structure:
$$T_{e}\,\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}\;\cong\;H^{1}(C,\mathcal{O}_{C}),\qquad \dim=g.$$
Remaining: the $k$-linear comparison $\operatorname{Dual}_k(\mathfrak{m}_e/\mathfrak{m}_e^{2})\cong H^{1}(C,\mathcal O_C)$ (`tangentSpaceIso`); then un-invert `smooth` and `proper` (ChevalleyвҖ“Rosenlicht) and geometric irreducibility.

- AJC.picrep [active/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Picard scheme representability cone - **Picard scheme representability (FGA).** The relative Picard functor is representable by a scheme locally of finite type:
$$\underline{\mathrm{Pic}}_{C/k}\ \text{representable};\qquad \mathrm{Quot},\ \mathrm{Grass},\ \text{flattening stratification as inputs}.$$
Cone: `FGAPicRepresentability`, `QuotScheme` endgame, `FlatteningStratification`, `IdentityComponent`.

- AJC.substrate [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Foundational substrate (KГӨhler, rigidity, AbelвҖ“Jacobi) - **Foundational substrate (sorry-free).** KГӨhler differentials / cotangent ($\Omega_{C/k}$, cotangent iso, chart algebra), the rigidity lemma, genus theory, AbelвҖ“Jacobi scaffolding, and line-bundle coherence/pullback (`RelPicFunctor`, `RelativeSpec`).

- ALB.ab [done/proof/normal] (Albanese) AuslanderвҖ“Buchsbaum / coheight bridge - **AuslanderвҖ“Buchsbaum / coheight bridge (sorry-free).** `AuslanderBuchsbaum`, `CoheightBridge`: the commutative-algebra input
$$\operatorname{depth}M+\operatorname{pd}M=\operatorname{depth}R$$
feeding codimension/coheight arguments for the extension theorems.

- ALB.codim1 [active/proof/normal] (Albanese) Codim-one & Thm 3.2 rational-map extension - **Codim-one & Thm 3.2 rational-map extension.** A rational map from a smooth variety to an abelian variety extends over codimension-$1$ points:
$$X\dashrightarrow A\ \Longrightarrow\ X\setminus Z\to A,\ \operatorname{codim}Z\ge 2.$$
`CodimOneExtension` (Г—3), `Thm32RationalMapExtension` (Г—2).

- ALB.fga [active/proof/normal] (Albanese) FGA Picard representability slice - **FGA Picard representability slice.** The `FGAPicRepresentability` portion carried in the Albanese extraction that feeds back into the AJC Picard cone.

- ALB.up [active/proof/high] (Albanese) Albanese universal property of Pic^0 - **Albanese universal property of $\mathrm{Pic}^{0}$** (Milne III В§6, Prop 6.1). Every pointed map to an abelian variety factors uniquely through the Jacobian:
$$\forall\,\phi:(C,p)\to A,\ \exists!\ \bar{\phi}:\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}\to A\ \text{with}\ \phi=\bar\phi\circ\mathrm{aj}.$$
`AlbaneseUP` (Г—7): the headline `Pic.albaneseUP` + universal-map descent.

- CECH.acyclic [done/proof/normal] (Cech-Cohomology) Affine acyclicity (Serre vanishing) - **Affine acyclicity (Serre vanishing).**
$$\check{H}^{i}(\mathfrak{U},\mathcal{F})=0\ (i>0)\quad\text{on affine opens},$$
via tilde-vanishing $\Rightarrow$ affine ДҢech vanishing; localization comparison `AwayComparison`, $\varphi/\varphi_L$ naturality.

- CECH.engine [done/proof/normal] (Cech-Cohomology) Combinatorial / free ДҢech engine - **Combinatorial / free ДҢech engine (sorry-free).** Alternating coface complex, homotopy contraction, and exactness of the augmented ДҢech complex on a cover.

- CECH.main [done/proof/high] (Cech-Cohomology) cech_computes_higherDirectImage (unconditional) - **Comparison theorem `cech_computes_higherDirectImage`.**
$$H^{i}\big(\check{C}^{\bullet}(\mathfrak{U},\mathcal{F})\big)\;\cong\;R^{i}f_{*}\mathcal{F}$$
for separated quasi-compact $f$ and a finite affine cover вҖ” unconditional (no enough-injectives appeal). Deliverable merged into AJC.

- CECH.pushpull [done/proof/normal] (Cech-Cohomology) PushPull functoriality - **PushPull functoriality.** `pushPullMap` composition, leg coherence, and the pentagon for the sigma iso over the ДҢech nerve / wide pullbacks (finitary-extensive distributivity).

- GRQ.cells [done/proof/normal] (GR-Quot-Closure) Grassmannian cells, gluing & descent - **Grassmannian cells, gluing & descent (sorry-free).** Rank-quotient setoid, affine charts, the $\mathrm{GL}_{d}$ transition cocycle, and effective descent gluing the charts into $\mathrm{Grass}(V,d)$.

- GRQ.graded [blocked/proof/normal] (GR-Quot-Closure) Section graded ring (SNAP) - **Section graded ring (SNAP, sorry-free).** The graded ring/module lanes (Stacks 01CV) and graded HilbertвҖ“Serre rationality:
$$\sum_{n}\dim_{k}M_{n}\,t^{n}\in\mathbb{Z}(t)\ \text{(rational)}.$$

- GRQ.quot [done/proof/normal] (GR-Quot-Closure) Quot scheme (quasi-coherent descent) - **Quot scheme (quasi-coherent descent, sorry-free).** The quasi-coherent-descent machinery of `QuotScheme` retained after carving the ПҮ-blocked Hilbert-polynomial endgame (which stays open in the AJC copy).

- GRQ.represents [blocked/proof/high] (GR-Quot-Closure) Grassmannian.represents (H^0 leg) - **Representability `Grassmannian.represents` (H^0 leg).**
$$\mathrm{Grass}(V,d)\ \text{represents}\ T\mapsto\{\,V_{T}\twoheadrightarrow Q\ \text{rank }d\,\},\qquad \texttt{tautologicalQuotient\_epi}.$$

- LBC.compinv [done/proof/high] (Line-Bundle-Comparison-Iso) Terminal comparison inverse (Pic^sharp group law) - **Terminal comparison inverse (deliverable вҖ” finished).** The keystone `trivialisation_restrict_compat` and the chart-chase inverse, giving the comparison isomorphism that endows $\mathrm{Pic}^{\sharp}_{C/k}$ with its group structure. Merges back into AJC (**T1**).

- LBC.dual [done/proof/normal] (Line-Bundle-Comparison-Iso) Slice-dual transport iso (DUAL route) - **Slice-dual transport iso (DUAL route, sorry-free).**
$$\mathcal{L}^{\vee}|_{U}\;\cong\;(\mathcal{L}|_{U})^{\vee}$$
compatibly across the trivializing slices вҖ” `DualInverse`, `DualInverse/SliceTransport`.

- LBC.pullback [done/proof/normal] (Line-Bundle-Comparison-Iso) Line-bundle pullback / relative Pic functor - **Line-bundle pullback / relative Pic functor (sorry-free).** `LineBundlePullback`, `RelPicFunctor`; the seed compatibility $f^{*}(\mathcal L\otimes\mathcal M)\cong f^{*}\mathcal L\otimes f^{*}\mathcal M$ on locally trivial bundles (`pullback_tensor_iso_loctriv`).

- LBC.stalktensor [done/proof/normal] (Line-Bundle-Comparison-Iso) Stalk-tensor / internal-hom machinery - **Stalk-tensor / internal-hom machinery (sorry-free).** `TensorObjSubstrate/StalkTensor`, `PresheafInternalHom` вҖ” the local model for $\otimes$ and $\mathcal{H}om$ on quasi-coherent sheaves used by the comparison.

- MR.pic [pending/blueprint/normal] (MR0555258-Compactifying-Picard) Compactifying the Picard scheme (D'Souza) - **Compactifying the Picard scheme** (D'Souza, *MR0555258*). Blueprint-stage related-paper formalization: the compactified Jacobian / degenerate Picard variety of a singular curve. Depends on the core AG engine (schemes, cohomology, Picard). Lean target is currently a stub aggregator.

# Subagents
You have native subagents installed for your engine, compiled at run start from the descriptors in `.archon-horizon/subagents/<name>.md` (Claude reads `.claude/agents/`, Codex reads `.codex/agents/`):
- blueprint вҖ” Own one assigned blueprint slice end-to-end вҖ” keep it pure math with complete proofs, cite references with \source{...} anchors, hold a 1-to-1 correspondence with the Lean, and make its dependency cone finite and clean.
- debug вҖ” Diagnose and repair workspace setup/build/environment failures during a run вҖ” missing toolchain, broken lakefile, MCP/config/env problems, obscure non-Lean-syntax errors вҖ” applying safe fixes or reporting a precise diagnosis.
- janitor вҖ” Workspace-hygiene auditor and tidier вҖ” keeps the workspace and each subproject navigable, READMEs/roadmap concise and current, files findable, and the inbox from overflowing (capping memory tags, closing what's done).
- page-transcriber вҖ” Transcribe a small PDF page range (max 5 pages) into faithful per-page LaTeX using AI VISION on rendered page images (never OCR), each page self-contained with the operators/macros it declares.
- reference-retriever вҖ” Fetch original math references into workspace-root references/, register them in references/manifest.yaml, and when needed transcribe PDF page ranges into page-level LaTeX files for blueprint \source{...} anchors.
- work-reviewer [read-only] вҖ” Fresh-context review of the previous Horizon agent's work on a task вҖ” starts from its git diff, its report, and the task it was given, but is free to dig deeper вҖ” then judges whether the work is converging and files concise inbox issues/memory for stuck points and errors.
- blueprint-reviewer [read-only] вҖ” Check that the Lean declarations match their blueprint statements and the \uses dependency edges are correct.
- diff-auditor [read-only] вҖ” Audit the project diff from the last Horizon round for sound reasoning, no stray files, and no buried sorries.

Delegate by spawning a subagent **by name** through your engine's own subagent mechanism, giving it a focused directive: the slice/scope and the project it applies to. Spawn several in parallel when the work divides cleanly, then wait for and reconcile their reports. Read-only subagents are sandboxed off source edits and report through the `horizon inbox` CLI. You decide whether a subagent fits вҖ” use them when they help, skip them when they don't. Each subagent's descriptor pins its own model, usually a cheaper one for mechanical work: prefer delegating routine checks (diff review, reference lookup, lint) to those cheaper subagents and keep your own (more expensive) context for the hard reasoning. To add or change a subagent, edit its descriptor under `.archon-horizon/subagents/` вҖ” it recompiles on the next run.

name: project-git
description: How git works in an Archon Horizon workspace вҖ” root commits include scoped project files plus shared state, while each project also has an out-of-tree VCS journal for detailed per-round history.
---

Read this before running `git diff`/`git log` on a project.

## The model

- **Workspace repo is out-of-tree too** вҖ” its git dir is
  `.archon-horizon/vcs/workspace.git` with the workspace **root** as its work tree
  (driven via `--git-dir`/`--work-tree`). **`<root>/.git`** is deliberately not touched, 
  because it is reserved for a user repo. It commits `config.yaml`, scoped project files, and `.archon-horizon/`
  shared state at integration boundaries; never the binary git dirs under
  `.archon-horizon/vcs/`, locks, or other volatile internals.
- **Project repos live out-of-tree.** Every project should have an out-of-tree
  git directory at `.archon-horizon/vcs/<project>.git`, with the project
  directory as its work tree. Project directories do not contain nested `.git/`.
- Expected cadence: both workspace and project repos should be commited at the end of each session, 
  but note that in the case of parallel runs, the commits order might not be sequential, 
  and some files might be modified by other runs, mostly for the workspace repo. However, 
  this should not be a problem in general. 

So a plain `git diff` at the root sees only a user repo (if any), never Archon's.
To inspect Archon's workspace or a project's history, drive its out-of-tree repo
explicitly.

```bash
# Workspace (manifest) history:
git --git-dir=.archon-horizon/vcs/workspace.git --work-tree=. log --oneline -n 20
```

## Reading a project's history / diff

Run from the workspace root:

```bash
GD=.archon-horizon/vcs/<project>.git
WT=<path-to-project>      # the project's `path` from the project list

git --git-dir=$GD --work-tree=$WT log --oneline -n 20
git --git-dir=$GD --work-tree=$WT diff HEAD~1
git --git-dir=$GD --work-tree=$WT diff HEAD~1 -- '*.lean'
git --git-dir=$GD --work-tree=$WT show <sha>
```

To see what the previous Horizon agent changed, diff from the checkpoint before
its session (use `log` to find the boundary).

## Caveats

- Every configured project is expected to have VCS enabled. If
  `.archon-horizon/vcs/<project>.git` is missing, report it as a workspace setup
  issue rather than treating it as normal, but work with it, it might be a new project, or a project that was cloned from the workspace's repo (hence without the vcs committed).
- You read history; you do not commit manually. Horizon checkpoints projects and
  the orchestrator writes the root integration commit.
- The bundled author identity is `Archon Horizon <archon-horizon@local>`.

 \subsection*{\texttt{BareScheme.lean}: the bare scheme \(\mathbb P^1\) and its presentation supplement}
-
+\begin{lemma}\leanok
+[The two standard charts cover \(\mathbb P^1\)]
+  \label{lem:projectiveLineBarAffineCover_span}
+  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.projectiveLineBarAffineCover_span}
+  \uses{def:genus0_base_objects}
+  For the standard grading of \(\bar k[X_0, X_1]\), the irrelevant ideal is contained in the
+  ideal generated by \(\{X_0, X_1\}\). Equivalently, the two basic opens \(D_+(X_0)\) and
+  \(D_+(X_1)\) cover \(\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}\).
+\end{lemma}
+\begin{proof}\leanok
+  An element \(p\) of the irrelevant ideal has vanishing degree-\(0\) homogeneous component,
+  so it is a sum of monomials \(r\,X^d\) with multi-index \(d \neq 0\). For each such monomial
+  some coordinate \(d_j > 0\) (\(j \in \{0,1\}\)), whence \(X_j \mid X^d\) and \(r\,X^d\) lies
+  in \((X_0, X_1)\); summing, \(p \in (X_0, X_1)\). Passing to \(\mathrm{Proj}\), the vanishing
+  loci of \(X_0\) and \(X_1\) have empty common intersection, so their basic opens cover.
+\end{proof}
 
 \subsection*{\texttt{ChartIso.lean}: the chart-ring iso \(\mathrm{Away}\,\mathcal A\,(X_i) \cong \bar k[u]\)}
-
+\begin{definition}\leanok
+[The chart-ring iso \(\mathrm{Away}\,\mathcal A\,(X_i) \cong \bar k[u]\)]
+  \label{def:homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso}
+  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso}
+  \uses{def:genus0_base_objects}
+  For \(i \in \{0, 1\}\) there is a \(\bar k\)-algebra isomorphism
+  \[ \mathrm{Away}\,\mathcal A\,(X_i) \;\cong\; \bar k[u] \]
+  from the degree-\(0\) part of the homogeneous localization of \(\bar k[X_0, X_1]\) away from
+  \(X_i\) onto the one-variable polynomial ring \(\bar k[u]\). It realises, at the ring level,
+  the standard identification of the affine chart \(D_+(X_i) \subseteq \mathbb P^1\) with
+  \(\mathbb A^1\): the forward map sends the degree-\(0\) generator \(X_{1-i}/X_i\) to \(u\), and
+  the inverse evaluates \(u \mapsto X_{1-i}/X_i\); the two composites are the identity, checked on
+  the underlying localization.
+\end{definition}
+\begin{lemma}\leanok
+[Chart ring stays a domain after base change]
+  \label{lem:chartAway_tensor_isDomain}
+  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.chartAway_tensor_isDomain}
+  \uses{def:homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso}
+  Let \(K/\bar k\) be a field extension and \(i \in \{0, 1\}\). Then the base change of the chart
+  ring to \(K\),
+  \[ \mathrm{Away}\,\mathcal A\,(X_i) \otimes_{\bar k} K, \]
+  is an integral domain.
+\end{lemma}
+\begin{proof}\leanok
+  By the chart-ring iso (\cref{def:homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso}), which is a
+  \(\bar k\)-algebra isomorphism, tensoring with \(K\) over \(\bar k\) gives
+  \[ \mathrm{Away}\,\mathcal A\,(X_i) \otimes_{\bar k} K \;\cong\; \bar k[u] \otimes_{\bar k} K
+     \;\cong\; K[u], \]
+  the last isomorphism being the standard identification of the base change of a polynomial ring.
+  As \(K\) is a field, \(K[u]\) is an integral domain, and integrality transports along the ring
+  isomorphism.
+\end{proof}
+\begin{lemma}\leanok
+[Each chart is geometrically irreducible over \(\bar k\)]
+  \label{lem:chartAway_geomIrred}
+  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.chartAway_geomIrred}
+  \uses{lem:chartAway_tensor_isDomain}
+  For \(i \in \{0, 1\}\), the chart structure morphism
+  \(\Spec\bigl(\mathrm{Away}\,\mathcal A\,(X_i)\bigr) \to \Spec \bar k\) (the composite of the
+  chart open immersion \(D_+(X_i) \hookrightarrow \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}\) with the structure
+  morphism of \(\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}\)) is geometrically irreducible.
+\end{lemma}
+\begin{proof}\leanok
+  The composite equals \(\Spec\) of the algebra map
+  \(\bar k \to \mathrm{Away}\,\mathcal A\,(X_i)\). For any field extension \(K/\bar k\) the base
+  change along \(\Spec K \to \Spec \bar k\) is
+  \(\Spec\bigl(\mathrm{Away}\,\mathcal A\,(X_i) \otimes_{\bar k} K\bigr)\), and by
+  \cref{lem:chartAway_tensor_isDomain} this ring is an integral domain, so its prime spectrum is
+  irreducible. As the base change is irreducible for every \(K\), the morphism is geometrically
+  irreducible.
+\end{proof}
+\begin{lemma}\leanok
+[A space covered by two intersecting irreducible opens is irreducible]
+  \label{lem:irreducibleSpace_of_two_irreducible_opens}
+  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.irreducibleSpace_of_two_irreducible_opens}
+  Let \(\alpha\) be a topological space with open subsets \(U, V\) such that \(U\) and \(V\) are
+  each irreducible, \(U \cap V \neq \varnothing\), and \(U \cup V = \alpha\). Then \(\alpha\) is
+  irreducible.
+\end{lemma}
+\begin{proof}\leanok
+  Fix \(w \in U \cap V\); in particular \(\alpha\) is non-empty. To prove \(\alpha\)
+  preirreducible, let \(u, v\) be non-empty open subsets; we show \(u \cap v \neq \varnothing\).
+  \emph{Claim:} any non-empty open \(u'\) meeting \(U\) also meets \(V\). Indeed, since \(U\) is
+  irreducible and both \(u' \cap U\) and \(V \cap U\) (the latter contains \(w\)) are non-empty
+  relative opens of \(U\), they intersect, yielding a point of \(u' \cap V\). Now take any point
+  \(a \in u\): as \(U \cup V = \alpha\), either \(a \in V\) (so \(u\) meets \(V\)) or \(a \in U\)
+  (so \(u\) meets \(U\), hence meets \(V\) by the claim); either way \(u \cap V \neq \varnothing\).
+  Likewise \(v \cap V \neq \varnothing\). Finally, since \(V\) is irreducible and \(u \cap V\),
+  \(v \cap V\) are non-empty relative opens of \(V\), they intersect, giving a point of
+  \(u \cap v\).
+\end{proof}
+\begin{definition}\leanok
+[The generic point of \(\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}\)]
+  \label{def:projectiveLineBarGenericPt}
+  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.projectiveLineBarGenericPt}
+  \uses{def:genus0_base_objects}
+  The generic point of \(\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}\) is the zero homogeneous prime \(\langle 0 \rangle\)
+  of the domain \(\bar k[X_0, X_1]\); it is a prime not containing the irrelevant ideal (as
+  \(X_0 \neq 0\)), hence a genuine point of \(\mathrm{Proj}\).
+\end{definition}
+\begin{lemma}\leanok
+[The generic point lies in every basic open]
+  \label{lem:projectiveLineBar_genericPt_mem_basicOpen}
+  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.projectiveLineBar_genericPt_mem_basicOpen}
+  \uses{def:projectiveLineBarGenericPt}
+  For any nonzero homogeneous \(f \in \bar k[X_0, X_1]\), the generic point lies in the basic open
+  \(D_+(f)\); indeed the zero ideal contains no nonzero \(f\).
+\end{lemma}
+\begin{lemma}\leanok
+[\(\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}\) is geometrically irreducible over \(\Spec \bar k\)]
+  \label{lem:projectiveLineBar_geomIrred}
+  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.projectiveLineBar_geomIrred}
+  \uses{lem:chartAway_geomIrred, lem:irreducibleSpace_of_two_irreducible_opens,
+    lem:projectiveLineBarAffineCover_span, def:projectiveLineBarGenericPt,
+    lem:projectiveLineBar_genericPt_mem_basicOpen, def:genus0_base_objects}
+  The structure morphism \((\mathtt{ProjectiveLineBar}\,\bar k).\mathrm{hom} \colon
+  \mathbb P^1_{\bar k} \to \Spec \bar k\) is geometrically irreducible.
+\end{lemma}
+\begin{proof}\leanok
+  Let \(K/\bar k\) be a field extension; we must show the base change \(\mathbb P^1_K\) is
+  irreducible. Let \(p \colon \mathbb P^1_K \to \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}\) be the projection, and set
+  \(V_i := p^{-1}\bigl(D_+(X_i)\bigr)\) for \(i \in \{0, 1\}\).
+  \emph{Each \(V_i\) is irreducible.} The pullback of the chart open immersion
+  \(D_+(X_i) \hookrightarrow \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}\) along \(p\) has total space the base change to
+  \(K\) of the geometrically irreducible chart morphism (\cref{lem:chartAway_geomIrred}), hence an
+  irreducible space; \(V_i\) is its image (an open subset), so \(V_i\) is irreducible.
+  \emph{The \(V_i\) cover.} Since \(D_+(X_0) \cup D_+(X_1) = \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}\)
+  (\cref{lem:projectiveLineBarAffineCover_span}) and \(p\) maps into
+  \(\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}\), every point of \(\mathbb P^1_K\) lies in \(V_0\) or \(V_1\).
+  \emph{The \(V_i\) intersect.} The base field map \(\Spec K \to \Spec \bar k\) is surjective, so
+  its base change \(p\) is surjective; pick a preimage \(z_0\) of the generic point
+  (\cref{def:projectiveLineBarGenericPt}). By
+  \cref{lem:projectiveLineBar_genericPt_mem_basicOpen} the generic point lies in every basic open,
+  so \(p(z_0) \in D_+(X_i)\) and thus \(z_0 \in V_0 \cap V_1\).
+  By \cref{lem:irreducibleSpace_of_two_irreducible_opens}, \(\mathbb P^1_K\) is irreducible. As
+  this holds for every \(K\), the structure morphism is geometrically irreducible.
+\end{proof}
 
 \subsection*{\texttt{Points.lean}: standard \(\bar k\)-points and the groups \(\mathbb G_a\), \(\mathbb G_m\)}
+\begin{lemma}\leanok
+[\(\mathbb G_m\)'s coordinate ring stays reduced after base change]
+  \label{lem:gmRing_tensor_isReduced}
+  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.gmRing_tensor_isReduced}
+  \uses{def:genus0_base_objects}
+  For every field extension \(K/\bar k\), the base change of the coordinate ring of
+  \(\mathbb G_m\),
+  \[ \bar k[t, t^{-1}] \otimes_{\bar k} K, \]
+  is reduced.
+\end{lemma}
+\begin{proof}\leanok
+  Write \(\bar k[t, t^{-1}]\) as the localization of the polynomial ring \(P = \bar k[t]\) at the
+  powers of \(t\). Base change commutes with localization, so
+  \(\bar k[t, t^{-1}] \otimes_{\bar k} K\) is the localization of \(P \otimes_{\bar k} K\) at the
+  image of the powers of \(t\). Now \(P \otimes_{\bar k} K \cong K[t]\) is an integral domain,
+  hence reduced, and a localization of a reduced ring is reduced. Therefore
+  \(\bar k[t, t^{-1}] \otimes_{\bar k} K\) is reduced.
+\end{proof}
+\begin{lemma}\leanok
+[\(\mathbb G_m\) is geometrically reduced over \(\Spec \bar k\)]
+  \label{lem:gm_geometricallyReduced}
+  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.gm_geometricallyReduced}
+  \uses{lem:gmRing_tensor_isReduced, def:genus0_base_objects}
+  The structure morphism \((\mathbb G_m)_{\bar k} \to \Spec \bar k\) is geometrically reduced.
+\end{lemma}
+\begin{proof}\leanok
+  For each field extension \(K/\bar k\), the base change is
+  \(\Spec\bigl(\bar k[t, t^{-1}] \otimes_{\bar k} K\bigr) = \Spec\bigl(K[t, t^{-1}]\bigr)\), which
+  is reduced by \cref{lem:gmRing_tensor_isReduced}. As the base change is reduced for every \(K\),
+  the morphism is geometrically reduced.
+\end{proof}

name: leandag
description: Consult and refresh the blueprint dependency DAG (leandag) вҖ” declaration dependencies, cones, proved status, isolated/dangling nodes, verified union/intersection across projects вҖ” via `horizon leandag` and the cached DAG files.
---

The DAG links blueprint nodes by `\uses` edges. An edge `source -> target` means
*target depends on source*. The engine records each node's `\lean` link,
`proved`/`leanok` status, effort metrics, and Lean source metadata.

## Keep It Fresh

- Run the project build after Lean/blueprint edits so generated Lean artifacts and
  imported libraries are current.
- Run `horizon blueprint` after blueprint or Lean declaration changes; it writes
  `.archon-horizon/blueprints/<project>.json` for the dashboard and cached DAG
  consumers.
- Use `horizon leandag` for live inspection when deciding what to prove next.

## `horizon leandag` (preferred)

- `horizon leandag` вҖ” per-project summary: nodes, edges, proved count, and any
  isolated nodes (no edge in or out вҖ” usually a blueprint bug to fix).
- `horizon leandag -p <project> --node <label>` вҖ” one node's direct deps, full
  transitive cone, and reverse deps.
- `horizon leandag -p a -p b --union` вҖ” merge projects by declaration/node id.
  Same-id nodes are combined; divergent non-signature metadata is tolerated, but
  a Lean signature mismatch is an error to investigate.
- `horizon leandag -p a -p b --intersect` вҖ” only shared declaration/node ids,
  with the same signature check.
- Add `--json` for machine-readable output.

A node is "ready" when all dependencies are proved. Prefer ready nodes when
choosing what to attempt next.

## Cached JSON

`horizon blueprint` writes each project's DAG to
`.archon-horizon/blueprints/<project>.json` (`nodes`, `edges`, `dangling`) for
the dashboard. Under the leandag engine, `\uses` are resolved at build/cache
time, so `dangling` is empty there; the parser fallback still populates it. To
